supporting_webpages.description: The Center for Equity and Excellence in Teaching and Learning (CEETL) fosters an inclusive and welcoming teaching & learning community for faculty and the campus community to engage across disciplines. Our CEETL educational program offerings, initiatives, and resources promote learning environments that value diverse perspectives, experiences, and contributions. Through fostering partnerships across campus schools, departments, and offices, we provide institutional support at all levels of teaching and champion opportunities for reflection, connection, innovation, and inspiration.

supporting_messages.name: Echo Labs Trial (Video Captions) For BATV
supporting_messages.content:
Hey Dan,
 
Thanks for checking in. Please follow this link for relevant documentation.
 
Progress is slower than expected, but we’ve finished about 650 of the TV Archive's 3000 streaming clips, And all new clips are being added with captioning.
 
We think a realistic estimate is to finish about half of the remaining clips by the end of December 2025 and the rest by April-May 2026.
 
We're also hoping to start on a separate project in 2026 to provide Black American Sign Language support for selected clips featuring our Black Panther Party Collection. But this is a whole different level of access and will take time to implement. I'll keep you updated as that takes shape.
 
Please let me know if you have any questions.
 
Regards,
Alex

supporting_notes.name: TAR Process is automatically required for all purchases
supporting_notes.content:
-	TAR is required for any technology purchase, regardless of the dollar value. This includes physical/tangible technology and software technology.
-	There is a pre-approved list of items that are TAR exempt.
-	TAR process includes ATI, ITS, and DPRC portions. TAR may be part of the Procurement process for certain purchases, but Procurement is not involved in the in-depth review. We check to see what may be required within the ticket.
-	If the TAR ticket concludes that certain IT supplemental provisions are necessary, then Procurement will include those into the Service Agreement.
-	If we are unsure if something requires a TAR or not, then it is best to submit a TAR to check.

supporting_messages.name: ATI Goal "Training Process for Buyers"
supporting_messages.content:
Hi Daniel,

In addition to training for new buyers, I also provide monthly requisition training to requesters on campus who sign up. For both buyer and requester training, I do touch upon the TAR process. It is not quite as extensive or in-depth as the information you provided in the link though.

When TAR is brought up in trainings, what I inform to buyers and requesters is:

-	TAR is required for any technology purchase, regardless of the dollar value. This includes physical/tangible technology and software technology.
-	There is a pre-approved list of items that are TAR exempt.
-	TAR process includes ATI, ITS, and DPRC portions. TAR may be part of the Procurement process for certain purchases, but Procurement is not involved in the in-depth review. We check to see what may be required within the ticket.
-	If the TAR ticket concludes that certain IT supplemental provisions are necessary, then Procurement will include those into the Service Agreement.
-	If we are unsure if something requires a TAR or not, then it is best to submit a TAR to check.

The link you provided includes some very useful information. I may tweak my training a little to include some of it. This could be a page I share with people during the training.

I hope this information is helpful for your report. Let me know if you need anything else.

Service.description: Built on the foundations of the 2020 JEDI PIE Certificate that focused on anti-racist pedagogy, this new certificate will focus on culturally relevant and culturally responsive pedagogy. This second course builds a community of JEDI protagonists through approximately 8-10 hours of deep-dive into developing culturally responsive and inclusive teaching practices. Special attention will be paid to transparent teaching; accessibility and universal design for learning; microaggressions in teaching practices; equitable and inclusive assignments and assessments; and centering student voice, belonging and experience.
Service.title: JEDI Faculty Training

supporting_messages.name: UCorp - Bookstore Advisory Committee
supporting_messages.content:
Hi Daniel,

I hope you’ve been well.

I wanted to reach out regarding our previous conversation around our campus’ approach to course material adoption compliance/reporting and your interest in joining UCorp’s Bookstore Advisory Committee.

Brian Beatty, Bookstore Advisory Committee Chair, and I met this morning to plan for our Fall 2025 meeting, which we are hoping to have either early November or just before the Thanksgiving break.  Before the committee meets, we need to finalize our roster, so I am reaching out to you to confirm your interest.

We plan to focus on adoption compliance/reporting pertaining to accessibility and affordability.  The goal is to find ways to improve and formalize the communication with faculty around the requirement each term.  While the bookstore does all it can to contact faculty and follow up with those that are not in compliance, etc., we really need support from the campus to strengthen the message and importance of communicating selected materials (even if no materials are required) to our campus bookstore.  Years ago, something like this existed that went to all faculty possibly from the Provost’s office, but it has not existed in quite a few years.  

I believe this will tie in well to the oversight you have been asked to take on and you’d make a great addition to the group.

Please let me know and if you are interested, I will be in touch on how we add you officially to the committee.

Best,
Chris

supporting_messages.content:
From: Brian J Beatty

Thanks,
Brian
From: Jennifer Summit <jsummit@sfsu.edu>
Date: Sunday, September 25, 2016 at 7:29 PM
To: Brian J Beatty <bjbeatty@sfsu.edu>, Lori Beth Way <lbway@sfsu.edu>, Alvin N Alvarez
<aalvarez@sfsu.edu>, Keith J Bowman <kjbowman@sfsu.edu>, "Andrew T. Harris"
<a1harris@sfsu.edu>, Kenneth P Monteiro <monteiro@sfsu.edu>, Judith H Munter
<jhmunter@sfsu.edu>, Linda Oubre <loubre@sfsu.edu>
Cc: Mona Sagapolutele <monas@sfsu.edu>
Subject: spring 2017 textbook orders due October 14
Dear colleagues,
Textbook orders for spring 2017 are due October 14; please share this date with associate deans and
chairs. The attached memo reminds campuses that timely ordering of textbooks is important to
students receiving financial aid.
In addition, please convey the following information about our Affordable Instructional Materials
program (administered by Brian Beatty):
1. The Affordable Instructional Materials (AIM) Award program provides recognition and a
modest stipend to faculty who adopt less expensive instructional materials. In the past two
years, faculty recognized with AIM Awards have saved students an estimated $1,040,000.
Information and details about the AIM Award program are available
at http://affordablelearning.sfsu.edu/, or contact SF State’s AIM Coordinator, AVP Brian
Beatty at bjbeatty@sfsu.edu.
2. SF State will soon launch an Open Education Resources Adoption program funded by a grant
from the California College Textbook Affordability Act of 2015 (AB 798). The program will
provide faculty development opportunities and consultation with peers, instructional
designers and librarians to support faculty in identifying and reviewing instructional materials
alternatives that do not cost students additional money. More information about this
program is available at an upcoming lunch and learn event on October 12, 2016. For more
information, visit http://affordablelearning.sfsu.edu/events-opportunities
Thanks for your help getting the word out.
Best wishes,
Jennifer
Jennifer Summit
Interim Provost and Vice President of Academic Affairs
San Francisco State University
1600 Holloway Avenue
San Francisco CA 94132
